alternatives.name: RFID/BLE Wander Management (Stanley WanderGuard, CenTrak)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Per-resident wristband required — fails if resident removes it; requires ceiling-mounted door antennas; cannot independently satisfy NFPA 101 single-motion escape requirements; $250K–$600K+ full facility deployment; 9,000+ facility installed base

alternatives.name: Electromagnetic Delayed-Egress Locks (Assa Abloy, dormakaba)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Power-dependent — must have battery backup for fire-safe operation; NFPA 101 §7.2.1.6 limits delay to max 30 s; licensed electrician required for installation and wiring; disruptive retrofit in existing buildings

alternatives.name: IoT Emergency Exit Alarm Guards (GfS Türwächter) ← this product
alternatives.tradeoffs: Alarms but does not delay exit — cannot physically prevent elopement in the way a delayed-egress lock can; EasyWave protocol not native BACnet/KNX; ~€730 per door; pre-alarm false-trigger risk in high-traffic zones

alternatives.name: Coded Keypad Exits
alternatives.tradeoffs: No monitoring capability; NFPA 101 requires single-motion escape — keypad entry adds a step and may require AHJ approval; staff must memorize codes; codes expire or are shared

alternatives.name: Manual staff monitoring / do nothing
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero hardware cost; zero false alarms; inadequate overnight/weekend coverage; CMS F-tag immediate jeopardy exposure when elopement is documented; average elopement claim = $360,000
